comments.text:
👇 Everything we curated for this South China Sea episode. Books, history, ocean resources, travel essentials.

*📚 BROWSE THE SOUTH CHINA SEA COLLECTION*
  ◦ Every book and ocean resource we'd recommend for this episode → https://amzn.to/4fPBLlD

*🛍 SEE OUR FULL W.O.E. STOREFRONT*
  ◦ Every destination Collection in one place → https://www.amazon.com/shop/whereonearth

  ◦ Manila, Palawan, and Visayas tours → https://shorturl.at/z0zKr
  ◦ Browse all Philippines experiences → https://shorturl.at/XVHED

*📖 PLAN YOUR JOURNEY THROUGH THE REGION*
  ◦ Lonely Planet Philippines, current edition → https://amzn.to/4wYa1l4
  ◦ Lonely Planet Filipino (Tagalog) Phrasebook and Dictionary → https://amzn.to/4wWJnc3

*📚 UNDERSTAND THE SOUTH CHINA SEA*
  ◦ The South China Sea by Bill Hayton, Yale University Press → https://amzn.to/3PL2Ko0
  ◦ Asia's Cauldron by Robert D. Kaplan → https://amzn.to/3Qat0Iv

*📖 GO DEEPER INTO THE STORY*
  ◦ Catfish and Mandala by Andrew X. Pham, Kiriyama Prize winner → https://amzn.to/4fURcca

*🌊 EXPLORE THE OCEAN ITSELF*
  ◦ Coral Reefs: A Natural History by Charles Sheppard, Princeton → https://amzn.to/4virt20

comments.author: @livannal.t.9068
comments.text: speaking of south china sea, also look up the most notorious pirate you've never heard of, Ching Shih, not well-known like black beard but surpass him in success, lived a long life and even retired successfully from piracy having negotiating herself a peaceful transistion

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: Ching Shih commanded over 1,800 vessels at her peak — one of the most formidable naval forces in history, male or female. That story deserves its own film. Thank you for watching. 🌿
